response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced and technically versatile developer, but he is a fundamental mismatch for this role. The single most disqualifying factor is the hard requirement for native-level Japanese language proficiency — the candidate lists English, Romanian, and German, with no mention of Japanese. Beyond that, his 15-year career is overwhelmingly concentrated in web and backend development (TypeScript, Node.js, React, DLang), with no demonstrated expertise in embedded firmware, network security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X), Layer 2/3 switching, or Linux kernel modification. C programming appears only peripherally in his history, insufficient for the depth this role demands.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded domains
Demonstrated systems-level exposure, including C/C++ at National Instruments and a Linux/C++ audio synthesis project for his thesis
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) that would transfer to any engineering team
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — this is an explicit, non-negotiable hard requirement for the role
No experience with embedded networking security (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, switch chips, BIOS/bootloader, or chip-vendor SDKs)
Primary technical identity is web/backend (TypeScript, Node.js, MongoDB), with C listed nowhere in his skills section despite it being the core language for this position
